Dear Miss Cameron:
This is a request for an opinion from the FPPC as to whether
I have a financlal interest whlch mlght disqualify me from
participating as an office holder in decisions regarding the
Humboldt County Local
Coastal
Plan or the Humboldt County
General Planr
My office is County Supervisor, District Fiver
As my statement
of interest shows,
I reside at Eig lagoon, which is in the
coastal zone,
The cabin is on land owned by the Big Lagoon
Park Corporation, Inc,
By contract of purchase, my wife
and I are buying the cabin and a share of stock in the
corporation.
The stock allows its holder to have a residence
on corporation land.
Upon satisfaction of the terms of the
Contract of sale, my wife and 1 may become stockholders in
the corporation, with the approval of its heard of directors.
in the meanwhile, we are allowed to participate in shareholders'
meetings by assignment of proxy,
If we should sell our
home, we must meet all terms of the contract of purchase,
and any profit would belong to my wife and myself, or our
assignsr
The corporatlon land and our cabin is located within the
region considered by the Northcoast Area Plan which when
adopted will become s portion of the l-lumhcldt County local
Coastal
Plan and General
Plan,
Our equity 1“ the contract of purchase agreement on our home
and the stock is in excess of sl,nuo
- and less than sln,aon.

.

the land owned by the Big Lagoon Park Corporation, Inca

This is behind the prevailing prices for
similar property in our county. but does indicate some
response
to market Conditions and inflationr
I believe the
low price of this coastal property is attributable to the
horizontal segregation of interest in the land from interest
in the structure:
Corporation bylaws do not even allow
building fences, on the premise that such a rule is necessary
to discourage residents from feeling like they own a separate
lot.
We also own
a rental
house
in Eureka at 325 ”0" Street,
within the city limits and the coastal planning area,

is a first deed of trust outstanding on this real property,

sections.question: You had asked in an earlier [letter] whether your interest in real property at Blue Lagoon would require you to disqualify yourself from participating in decisions of the Humboldt County Board of Supervisors in regard to the Northeast Area Plan.
sections.conclusion: Since the Big Lagoon Park Corporation holds property along a stretch of coastal bluff, you have a potential conflict of interest as to the seismic ordinance issue. However, we refrain from giving advice to public officials with respect to past actions. Since the purpose of written advice is to give guidance as to future actions, we will not attempt to advise you as to the issues which have already been decided. It is understood that advice at this time would be premature because we do not yet know the precise question which will be put before the board. Furthermore, since this is basically a safety ordinance, the issue, as it finally comes before the Board, may pose no conflict of interest for you.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a County Supervisor's financial interest in property at Blue Lagoon (owned by Big Lagoon Park Corporation) requires disqualification from participating in Board decisions regarding the Northeast Area Plan and related coastal/general planning matters.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC identifies a potential conflict of interest regarding the seismic ordinance issue due to the corporation's coastal bluff property. However, the FPPC declines to provide advice on past decisions and considers premature to advise on the seismic ordinance until it is drafted and the precise question is known, as it may ultimately pose no conflict.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains two components: an incoming request letter (January 27, 1981) and FPPC response letter (April 10, 1981). Response letter has poor OCR quality affecting some readability. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections but substantive advice provided. Incoming request shows clear financial interest disclosure details.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You had asked in an earlier [letter] whether your interest in real property at Blue Lagoon would require you to disqualify yourself from participating in decisions of the Humboldt County Board of Supervisors in regard to the Northeast Area Plan.

CONCLUSION: Since the Big Lagoon Park Corporation holds property along a stretch of coastal bluff, you have a potential conflict of interest as to the seismic ordinance issue. However, we refrain from giving advice to public officials with respect to past actions. Since the purpose of written advice is to give guidance as to future actions, we will not attempt to advise you as to the issues which have already been decided. It is understood that advice at this time would be premature because we do not yet know the precise question which will be put before the board. Furthermore, since this is basically a safety ordinance, the issue, as it finally comes before the Board, may pose no conflict of interest for you.

embedding.summary: A Humboldt County Supervisor requested FPPC advice on whether his financial interest in property owned by Big Lagoon Park Corporation would disqualify him from participating in Board decisions on the Northeast Area Plan. The FPPC confirmed a telephone conversation declining to advise on issues already decided but identifying a potential conflict regarding an undrafted seismic ordinance issue.
